Name: EnterpriseRecommended
Description: A conservative checklist of important Windows health and security settings. EndpointForge can safely preview a small set of supported fixes; hardware protections are checked but never changed automatically.

Controls.Title: Domain firewall profile is enabled
Controls.Description: Requires Windows Firewall for the domain profile.
Controls.WhyItMatters: The firewall helps block unwanted network connections while this computer is connected to an organization network.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks Windows whether the Domain firewall profile is turned on.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: EndpointForge would turn on the Domain firewall profile. It would not replace firewall rules.
Controls.ManualAction: If an organization policy controls the firewall, ask your IT administrator to review that policy.
Controls.SafetyNotes: No restart is normally needed. Organization policy can override the local setting.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: If a confirmed application problem follows the change, use approved Windows Firewall policy or tools to restore the prior profile state shown in the change receipt.
Controls.Name: Domain

Controls.Title: Private firewall profile is enabled
Controls.Description: Requires Windows Firewall for the private profile.
Controls.WhyItMatters: The firewall helps block unwanted network connections on trusted home or work networks.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks Windows whether the Private firewall profile is turned on.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: EndpointForge would turn on the Private firewall profile. It would not replace firewall rules.
Controls.ManualAction: If an organization policy controls the firewall, ask your IT administrator to review that policy.
Controls.SafetyNotes: No restart is normally needed. Organization policy can override the local setting.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: If a confirmed application problem follows the change, use approved Windows Firewall policy or tools to restore the prior profile state shown in the change receipt.
Controls.Name: Private

Controls.Title: Public firewall profile is enabled
Controls.Description: Requires Windows Firewall for the public profile.
Controls.WhyItMatters: The firewall is especially important on public networks, where other connected devices may not be trusted.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks Windows whether the Public firewall profile is turned on.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: EndpointForge would turn on the Public firewall profile. It would not replace firewall rules.
Controls.ManualAction: If an organization policy controls the firewall, ask your IT administrator to review that policy.
Controls.SafetyNotes: No restart is normally needed. Organization policy can override the local setting.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: If a confirmed application problem follows the change, use approved Windows Firewall policy or tools to restore the prior profile state shown in the change receipt.
Controls.Name: Public

Controls.Title: User Account Control is enabled
Controls.WhyItMatters: User Account Control helps prevent programs from making administrator-level changes without your knowledge.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge reads the Windows setting that turns User Account Control on or off.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: EndpointForge would turn on User Account Control.
Controls.ManualAction: If this setting is controlled by your organization, ask IT to review its Windows security policy.
Controls.SafetyNotes: A restart is required before this change is fully active. Applications may begin asking for administrator approval when needed.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: Use the before value in the change receipt and your approved Windows security policy if an administrator determines the setting must be restored.

Controls.Title: Microsoft Defender real-time protection is enabled
Controls.Description: Requires real-time protection when Microsoft Defender is available.
Controls.WhyItMatters: Real-time protection scans files and activity for known threats while you use the computer.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks Microsoft Defender whether real-time protection is active.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: EndpointForge would ask Microsoft Defender to turn real-time monitoring on.
Controls.ManualAction: If another antivirus product or organization policy controls protection, ask your security administrator to review it.
Controls.SafetyNotes: Tamper protection, another antivirus product, or organization policy may prevent or later replace the local change.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: If a security administrator confirms the change must be reversed, use the prior state in the receipt and the approved antivirus management tool.

Controls.Title: SMB 1.0 optional feature is disabled
Controls.Description: Requires the legacy SMB1 optional feature to be disabled when present.
Controls.WhyItMatters: SMB 1.0 is an obsolete file-sharing protocol with serious security weaknesses.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks Windows whether the SMB 1.0 optional feature is enabled.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: EndpointForge would disable the SMB 1.0 Windows optional feature.
Controls.ManualAction: Replace or isolate devices that still require SMB 1.0 before disabling it across an organization.
Controls.SafetyNotes: A restart may be required. Very old file servers, printers, or applications may stop connecting.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: Do not re-enable SMB 1.0 casually. If a critical legacy dependency is confirmed, follow your organization's exception process and use the receipt to identify the prior state.
Controls.Name: SMB1Protocol

Controls.Title: Windows Update service is not disabled
Controls.Description: Requires the Windows Update service to use manual trigger start.
Controls.WhyItMatters: Windows needs this service available to discover and install approved security and reliability updates.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge reads the Windows Update service start setting.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: EndpointForge would set the service to Manual, allowing Windows to start it when needed. It would not install updates.
Controls.ManualAction: If updates are managed centrally, confirm the service setting with the team that manages Windows updates.
Controls.SafetyNotes: This change does not download or install updates and normally does not require a restart.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: If an administrator confirms a different start setting is required, restore the before value shown in the receipt through the approved service or device-management policy.
Controls.Name: wuauserv

Controls.Title: BitLocker protects the operating system volume
Controls.Description: Audits BitLocker protection. Encryption is never enabled automatically.
Controls.WhyItMatters: BitLocker helps protect files if a computer or its drive is lost or stolen.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks Windows whether BitLocker protection is active on the operating system drive.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: Nothing. EndpointForge never turns on drive encryption automatically.
Controls.ManualAction: Use your organization's approved BitLocker process so the recovery key is stored safely before encryption is enabled.
Controls.SafetyNotes: Enabling encryption without a recovery-key plan can cause permanent data loss. This item is report-only.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: No automatic change is made. Follow your organization's BitLocker recovery process if access is lost.

Controls.Title: Secure Boot is enabled
Controls.Description: Audits Secure Boot on supported UEFI devices.
Controls.WhyItMatters: Secure Boot helps prevent untrusted software from loading before Windows starts.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks supported firmware whether Secure Boot is active.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: Nothing. EndpointForge never changes firmware settings.
Controls.ManualAction: Ask IT or the computer manufacturer for the approved firmware procedure if Secure Boot should be enabled.
Controls.SafetyNotes: Changing firmware settings can prevent a computer from starting. This item is report-only.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: No automatic change is made. Use the manufacturer's recovery procedure for any firmware problem.

Controls.Title: Trusted Platform Module is present and ready
Controls.Description: Audits TPM presence and readiness. TPM state is never changed automatically.
Controls.WhyItMatters: A Trusted Platform Module stores security keys in protected hardware and supports features such as BitLocker and Windows Hello.
Controls.HowChecked: EndpointForge asks Windows whether a TPM is present and ready for use.
Controls.WhatWouldChange: Nothing. EndpointForge never initializes, clears, or changes a TPM.
Controls.ManualAction: Ask IT or the computer manufacturer to review firmware and TPM readiness. Never clear a TPM without confirming recovery keys and impact.
Controls.SafetyNotes: Clearing or resetting a TPM can make encrypted data unavailable. This item is report-only.
Controls.RecoveryGuidance: No automatic change is made. Follow the device and encryption recovery procedures approved by your organization.
